transcript.text: Hi there.
Hey, how's it going?
Good, how are you?
Doing all right.
Coming off some back-to-backs here.
Same.
Okay, good.
Thursdays and Wednesdays are my back-to-back meeting.
Somehow it all lined up for me on Thursday.
Like I'm just always, it's just a full back-to-back.
Mm-hmm.
Got that.
I don't know if Sydney might join.
She's on a plane.
Oh, okay.
All good.
I think we were, I felt like we were just on with her yesterday.
AsERior.
of
We sent over so much stuff on Slack, and then we also spoke Tuesday, including the imagery, so I'm going to for that.
okay, cool, cool.
Should we be waiting for Spencer?
Let me just see where he's at.
We were on an earlier call together, and he was peeking his, yeah, this is what he should be doing here.
Hang on, let me just go peek in and see where he's got.
I think he's still on that call.
I think we can get started, and then if he pops in, I'll just jump in there and join him.
Okay, okay, perfect.
Erik, got your note about Access.
I'll ping them again.
So you just still need those two, right?
It's just G and E, console, okay.
Yeah, those two.
Yeah, yeah.
We use it to set up your Looker dashboard.
So it's a six or seven page report that combines data from both platforms.
And we also use it to monitor the traffic performance of the blog posts that we published.
And there's also a page in that report that includes a breakdown of your LLM referral traffic.
So you can see which LLMs are driving the most traffic to your site.
And you can also see which webpages are getting the most traffic from LLMs.
So it's pretty cool.
And for a lot of our clients, their blogs attract a lot of users, which obviously, like, the homepage gets a lot of traffic.
But usually, like, the blog is, like, up somewhere in, like, in the top 10.
Yeah, let me just go in and...
So re-ping everyone here.
But you're on Framer, right?
Yes.
Okay.
I just re-bumped that for the IT folks.
Sometimes it just takes a couple of requests here.
There's a lot going on.
Sure.
Okay.
So before we get into the production update, I wanted to talk images.
I know that Katya, she met with Hudson and Ben two days ago.
She kind of, they told her kind of what is the idea, what it would like for us to do.
And the images that you shared as examples, they are images that are very, very detailed.
So they're almost, you know, infographics.
They have visuals and texts and charts and tables.
And she
She's going to experiment to see what's possible and how far we can get the workflow, what kind of images we can get.
So she's going to push it to see how far we can get, but it is a little bit, those types of diagrams are very, very complex for AI to do, so we'll keep you posted on our progress.
Okay.
Yeah.
Just keep us in the loop there.
Yeah, yeah, for sure.

And I mean, you know, I know that you, especially Spencer, he left a note saying that, you he does want images and blog content because it's, you know, a marker of human involvement, which is an important point.
We do want to publish just in the interest of collecting early signals.
We do want to publish as quickly as possible.
So as soon as a blog post is ready, we feel like it's good to go.
We want to publish it on your site.
Even if it might not have an image yet, we can go back in and like.
At it later.
We just don't want to, you know, spend any time waiting that we could be using to collect like traffic and see what's working.
Yeah.
Okay.
Perfect.
So in terms of the production update, so Sydney took in all the feedback that you shared on the first calibration blog.
She read the artifacts that we originally sent over.
And we have another article that's ready for your review.
We would love for you to kind of do a pass on it before we finalize the remaining two this week.
So this week we want to deliver three and the next week we want to deliver five.
So the calibration article is ready for another look.
There's an image here that we added and I think Sydney created this one manually.
She can, like, I think she's using, like, an infographic tool.
So just just to kind of give you something that you can publish.
But they should be more, the tone should be more direct, kind of less, less fluffy.
I think that was one of the comments that kind of came up for the first draft.
Okay, so we'll go through and then review the second draft for this one.
All right, great, all right.
And then I had dropped in some questions around like cross-linking and like the TLDR sections.
Just for me, like in terms of formatting, will you all be inserting the cross-links before we publish?
Yes, yeah, we do add internal links to every blog post that we draft.
Some of this process is automated.
We have like an internal linking step in our article generation pipeline.
But while we're editing, we also add links manually.
If you, this is more relevant for a
Like, really big websites, but sometimes clients will give us a spreadsheet with links that they want us to prioritize for internal linking.
But this is more helpful when the website has thousands of pages and they want to prioritize, like, specific pages for SEO.
Yeah, at this point, we're just trying to build up some stuff.
Okay, but it's good to know just for process because that does take a little time.
Okay, good.
Yeah, yeah.
And the TLDR, yeah, we added that here.
We're going to be adding it to all blog posts moving forward.
So, yeah, I think, is there anything that kind of stood out to you in the first draft that you didn't leave a comment on?
Do you have, like, any, like, meta comments on, like, the tone?
I'm just, because I might go in and, like, do a second pass on the writing guidelines and I would love to hear any additional feedback.
No, I think Spencer gave, like, extensive feedback.
We started to go through that together and then he just kind of...
Took it even further.
So I would say he originated a lot of the blogs on the site.
And then I was, you know, I think I added 10 or so.
I have, you know, a few draft.
And getting that level of feedback from him, like, yeah, that's the way to go.
He wants everything done.
But the way that I had kind of developed the voice and tone, I think I sent you all like the brand guidelines.
We try and just use, you know, plain English, and we try to be direct, authoritative, have that good mix.
It felt like the voice and tone was there and came across with the way that, you know, you had written it.
It's just that on, I think, the specific, like, technical details and things like that, this is where Spencer is giving a lot of, yeah, a lot of concrete direction there.
Yeah, yeah, that's super helpful.
I mean, yeah, whenever you're, you have an SME reviewing content, there's always going to be a lot of feedback and that's helpful.
It's just to refine the input, but yeah, it is helpful.
I love this comment, chefs rely on walks.
He left, we thought it was really helpful.
Okay.
Cool.
All right.
So all of those comments should be worked in.
And the new one that's ready for your review is what is risk supplier management?
Um, so yeah, we'll, be looking, looking out for your comments there.
Um, and then in terms of, uh, the glossary, I know we were talking about the glossary last week.
Um, so Sydney went ahead and did some research around just what are the general procurement glossaries that are out there.
Um, and she found, uh, this one from, um, the Institute of Procurement and Supply.
Okay, so she analyzed, um, what are the glossary items on their site?
Um, and she collected.
Like, she downloaded all of those keywords, and she uploaded them to the content OS.
Why is it?
I don't want to preview it.
I want to.
Never mind.
So I think you should be able to see this when you open the content OS.
When you group them by topic cluster, you should be able to see the glossary.
Hold on.
Just let me.
Let me open it again.
Sorry.
Just give me a second.
Okay.
So, yeah, in the glossary, there are 130 items in total.
I don't
I don't think all of them are going to be super high priority for you, but you can follow the same process as you did earlier and just approve or reject specific ideas.
I think the way we were thinking about publishing this is each glossary item would be a separate page just to maximize the impact that it could have on your website.
And the content, we would try to make sure that it's comprehensive enough so that it doesn't trigger any Google algorithms for thin content.
So they would be at least, I think, 600 or 800 words.
Well, that's great.
Yeah, that's much more than I was going to do.
I was really just going to publish that whole article, but that's amazing if we can get this full list of words.
Okay, so I'll have to
So through this, I have things that, you know, I would probably offhand approve easily, and then I'll just have Spencer take kind of a second cut as to what he thinks is super useful.
But a lot of these I can see, I think, are super useful to just have in.
Yeah, yeah.
And I mean, they're great for SEO.
They're great for building semantic authority.
You know, a lot of times, I think it's a natural question, like, why would we have this on our website?
Because our audience already knows, like, what is third-party risk management or, like, these types of concepts.
But it does help the robots understand that your website is an authority in a specific industry.
And the more information you have on your website about a specific topic, the more authority you have.
We have a client who is in the cybersecurity space, and they have a lot of content on their website.
So they have a blog set up where they...

Publish a lot of more product-related content, and it is human-written, and then they have a whole resource section of their website that's just very SEO content, so some of the stuff that we're wanting to publish, it's targeting specific keywords, and those blog posts are getting a lot of traffic from LLMs, and their AI visibility has been growing steadily, over the past 12 weeks, the more that they publish, because LLMs, they tend to surface websites that they view as answer-rich, so websites like Wikipedia, but at some point, if you have a lot of content on your site, LLMs start to see, even if it's a brand website, they will see your website as answer-rich.
So, it does work, but it does take, you know, a considerable amount of content on the site, you know, to start really, like, gaining a lot of, lot of traction, and...
Yeah, at that point, it's very obvious that it's working.
Got it.
Okay, good to know.
Yeah.
All right.
So then the big to-dos on our side, going through and knocking these out, reviewing the revved refresh article.
And the expectation is for this calibration article, we would then replace the current blog with this text.
Is that the best practice?
Yes.
So we would go in and just update the existing content.
Yeah.
Okay.
And then risk supplier management, brand new article.
Yeah, exactly.
That would be.
Yeah, I think, you know, any blog post that's older than a year should be at some point refreshed or optimized.
It obviously depends, like if it's a product announcement or if it's not, if it was never optimized for SEO, it's fine.
But if it's an SEO article and you really want to get some traffic, then it should be kind of every year it should get.
not, it's it's it's if it's it's
A little bit of a refresh.
Yeah.
And then do we need to go back in and refresh or review any net new ideas or are we set there?
Let me take a look by status.
So we have close to 60 blog ideas that are ready to start.
So we're in a really good spot.
Okay, great.
Yeah.
Okay.
Cool.
I think those are all the updates on my end.
Erik, did you have anything that you wanted to discuss?
I don't think so.
covered off on images, GSC, GA4 access, all the production updates.
So that's it.
Okay.
Yeah.
And just keep us in the loop on images.
Like, feel free to send over stuff that you have as it's getting done.
So Hudson, our lead designer, and I can rapidly respond to things to make sure you guys have the direction you need so we can get those going.
I don't want that to be there.
Go.
Go.
The lunch pin.
Okay, okay.
Okay, good stuff.
So all pinks, pins are in, just let them know that that's ready for re-review.
Or actually, if you can just drop that in the Slack and just mention it.
Yes.
That'll also kind of push him.
I think he's going to be on a flight later, so he'll be able to review.
And then I'll take first cut on the glossary terms, actually, with the rest of the time now.
Let's go ahead.
Okay, perfect.
Well, thank you for your time.
We'll see you next week.
And by then, I'm hoping we'll have the Looker Dashboard for your review, so we can go through it as well.
Same.
Okay.
Awesome.
Thanks, Ben.
Thanks, everyone.
Bye-bye.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.
Let's see.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content production, review feedback, and align on next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=82.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**GSC/GA4 access is blocked,** preventing a Looker dashboard that tracks key metrics, including LLM referral traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [**A 130-term procurement glossary is proposed** to build semantic authority and attract LLM traffic; each term will be a \~600-800 word page.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=630.0)
  - [**The "What is risk supplier management?" draft is ready for review;** the "calibration" article was updated with a more direct tone based on Spencer's feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=373.0)
  - [**Complex AI-generated images are a challenge;** content will be published without them to gather early traffic signals, with visuals added later.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=269.0)

## Topics

### Blocked: GSC & GA4 Access

  - [GrowthX needs GSC & GA4 access to build a Looker dashboard.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [**Purpose:** The dashboard will track key metrics, including a breakdown of LLM referral traffic (by LLM and webpage).](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=191.0)
  - [**Status:** Ben has re-pinged IT; GrowthX will also follow up on Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)

### Content Production & Review

  - [**Production Cadence:** 3 articles this week, 5 next week.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=390.0)
  - [**Review Items:**](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=373.0)
      - [**"Calibration" Article:** Updated with a more direct tone based on Spencer's feedback. Ben will review the revised draft.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=373.0)
      - [**"What is risk supplier management?" Article:** New draft ready for review.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=616.0)
  - [**Process Clarifications:**](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=441.0)
      - [**Internal Linking:** GrowthX will add links automatically and manually before publication.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=441.0)
      - [**TLDRs:** Will be added to all future articles.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)
      - [**Content Refresh:** SEO articles should be refreshed annually.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=935.0)

### AI-Generated Images

  - [**Challenge:** AI struggles with the complex, infographic-style images requested.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=269.0)
  - [**Interim Solution:** Publish articles without images to gather early traffic signals, then add visuals later.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=325.0)
  - [**Manual Backup:** Sydney created a simple infographic for the "calibration" article using a separate tool.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)

### Glossary Project

  - [**Proposal:** Create a 130-term procurement glossary, sourced from the Institute of Procurement and Supply.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=630.0)
  - [**Format:** Each term will be a separate page (\~600-800 words) to build semantic authority and avoid Google's "thin content" penalty.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=733.0)
  - [**SEO Rationale:** Builds topical authority, which helps attract LLM traffic. LLMs favor "answer-rich" sites (e.g., Wikipedia), a status a comprehensive glossary can help achieve.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=798.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Ben:**](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=907.0)
      - [Re-ping IT for GSC/GA4 access.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
      - [Review the revised "calibration" article.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=373.0)
      - [Review the new "What is risk supplier management?" article.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=616.0)
      - [Begin approving/rejecting glossary terms in the Content OS.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=1036.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=1006.0)
      - [Ping IT on Slack regarding GSC/GA4 access.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
      - [Keep Ben updated on AI image generation progress.](https://fathom.video/share/V3_nyucbFyx-czJTXefDM4ptattZF7sm?tab=summary&timestamp=1006.0)

action_items.description: Review 'What is risk supplier management?' in Content OS; send feedback to Aida
Review glossary in Content OS; approve/reject terms; send to Spencer for 2nd cut
Post Slack reminder to IT re: GSC/GA4 access for Erik
